_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: An urn contains 5 red balls and 3 green balls. One ball is drawn, its color is recorded, and it is returned to the urn. A second ball is then drawn. What is the probability that both draws are green?
author_rationale: The author marked 3/28 by computing (3/8)·(2/7), that is, by treating the second draw as happening over a reduced set. That contradicts the stem, which explicitly says the ball IS RETURNED to the urn.

intended_defect.description: The marked key (3/28) applies sampling without replacement even though the stem establishes replacement. With replacement the two draws are independent and the composition of the urn does not change.
intended_defect.expected_finding: The bounded solver must compute (3/8)·(3/8) = 9/64, contradicting the marked key. This item mirrors factual-error-002: there the author applied replacement where it did not belong, here the author drops the replacement that is explicitly stated.

source.excerpt: When a draw is made with replacement, the composition of the set is restored before the next draw and the resulting events are independent: P(A₁ ∩ A₂) = P(A₁) · P(A₂), with both factors computed over the same original set.
source.relevance: Establishes that both factors must be evaluated over the original 8 balls, which is exactly the step the author replaced with a reduced set.
